programme.desc: John Humphrys tests four more celebrities in the famous black chair. Taking part tonight Paralympic alpine skier Kelly Gallagher, comedian and presenter Stephen Bailey, actor and comedy writer Daniel Lawrence Taylor, and presenter and mathematician Bobby Seagull(n)

programme.title: Catchpoint
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness hosts a family-friendly game show combining physical fun and an exciting question mechanic, as contestants have to think fast as they answer questions while trying to catch balls of various sizes dropped from the studio ceiling, with a variety of entertaining rounds and a cash prize at stake(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Marty finally acknowledges how dangerous his behaviour has been outside of work and decides to change his ways. Meanwhile, Ethan questions what he really wants when Fenisha turns him down, and David suggests that Dylan's behaviour indicates feelings towards Faith, but Dylan denies it. Starring Shaheen Jafargholi and George Rainsford(n)

programme.title: Gavin & Stacey
programme.desc: First episode of the comedy about the romance between a couple with differing cultures and backgrounds. Gavin and Stacey have flirted for months on the phone at work but have never met up - until she travels from Wales to London to see him. Mathew Horne and Joanna Page star alongside Rob Brydon, Alison Steadman, Ruth Jones and James Corden(n)

programme.title: Outnumbered
programme.desc: The Brockmans have to make some tough decisions about work and video games, as well as who to take to a family funeral - an event that has the vicar wishing none of them had ever come. Comedy, starring Hugh Dennis, Claire Skinner and Tyger Drew-Honey(n)

programme.title: All Round to Mrs Brown's
programme.desc: Mrs Brown's Boys creator, writer and star Brendan O'Carroll dons Agnes's famous cardigan and curly wig once again as he throws open the doors of the larger-than-life Irish matriarch's home to host the Saturday night chat show. In the third episode of the series, Agnes and the family are joined on the show by Spice Girl and girl power icon Mel B, comedy actor and Little Britain star Matt Lucas, with his mother Diana Lobatto. Plus, a special musical performance from Jax Jones and Raye(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker is joined by Alan Shearer and Ian Wright for a discussion on the top ten goalkeepers in Premier League history(n)

programme.title: Source Code (2011)
programme.desc: A military pilot is the subject of a top-secret experiment that transfers his mind into the body of a man killed in a terrorist bombing on a train, but only for the last eight minutes of the dead man's life. His mission is to use that time to find out who is responsible and if they intend to strike again. Sci-fi thriller, starring Jake Gyllenhaal, Michelle Monaghan, Vera Farmiga and Jeffrey Wright(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Aled Jones speaks to Archbishop of York designate the Rt Rev Stephen Cottrell about the Christian hope that can still be found despite the current troubled times. Aled also refelcts on recent editions of the programme in which he met those whose faith saw them through especially challenging times - inlcuding a woman who survived the Grenfell Tower tragedy, and a Second World War veteran inolved in the D-Day landings(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: The programme returns to the waterfront beside the V&A Dundee design museum, with Fiona Bruce going aboard the Discovery to reveal the epic story of the ship and Antarctic explorer Robert Falcon Scott. Items brought in to be assessed by the experts include a self-pouring teapot, a glass helmet and a piece of tartan that has been to the Moon(n)

programme.title: Coast
programme.desc: The team investigates the culture, history and legends of Britain and its people, beginning with the nation's islands. Nick Crane learns how Fair Isle's small community has survived and Neil Oliver visits Orkney to explore conspiracy theories surrounding the death of Lord Kitchener. Tessa Dunlop heads for the Western Isles to investigate an optical phenomenon known as the green ray, Andy Torbet climbs a chalk pinnacle on the Isle of Wight, and folk singer June Tabor performs a traditional ballad(n)

programme.title: Hardball
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Ore Oduba in which contestants have to answer questions to get as far along a track as they can before a ball catches and passes them, ending their go(n)

programme.title: Put Your Money Where Your Mouth Is
programme.desc: Eric Knowles and Danny Sebastian visit Sint-Truiden in Belgium, where Danny finds an antique sewing machine and Eric finds out how modern bronzes are cast(n)

programme.title: Best Bakes Ever
programme.desc: In light of Britain's baking obsession, actress and cake expert Jane Asher presents a selection of recipes from television chefs, including James Martin, Mary Berry, Paul Hollywood, the Hairy Bikers, Raymond Blanc, Nigella Lawson, Tom Kerridge, Lorraine Pascale and Rachel Khoo(n)

programme.title: The TV That Made Me
programme.desc: Writer and comedy actor Adil Ray joins Brian Conley to discuss the shows of his youth, and to explain how the Liverpool-set Bread inspired him to create Asian sitcom Citizen Khan(n)

programme.title: Alex Polizzi: Chefs on Trial
programme.desc: The last three candidates are challenged to cook fish in a wood-fired oven as Alex continues her mission to find a head chef for Amelies seafood restaurant in Cornwall. Portuguese fisherwoman Celia takes a risk by serving up roasted octopus, Frenchman Fabien fails to tell his sea bass from his sea bream and Robert may be cool in the kitchen but struggles to take criticism. With only one place left in the final, the pressure is on(n)

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Charles Hanson and Michael Baggott assess objects brought into the University of Nottingham, while presenter Paul Martin learns about a Nottingham-born Victorian prize-fighter called Bendigo and meets locals who are keeping his memory alive(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Countdown's Susie Dent, newsreader Nick Owen, alpine ski racer Chemmy Alcott and comedian Dane Baptiste continue the daily test of their skills, hosted by Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: The vet is called when Violet the koala goes downhill, while Iggy Pop the iguana has a mystery tail injury that requires surgery - but at his age an operation is a risky business(n)

programme.title: Yorkshire Wolds Way with Paul Rose
programme.desc: The first of two programmes in which adventurer Paul Rose explores the 79 miles of the Yorkshire Wolds Way, one of Britain's least well-known national walking trails. The trail starts at the Humber Estuary and ends at the North Yorkshire seaside resort of Filey. On the way, Paul takes in the views from the top of the Humber Bridge, learns to ride a penny farthing and searches out the spots made internationally famous by the artist David Hockney(n)

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: Archaeologist Ben Robinson examines the history of the Lancashire village Sunderland Point, which is cut off twice a day by the tide and has become a haven for wildlife. He reveals it was once a busy port that was instrumental in the slave trade, and at the forefront of the luxury goods business that transformed 18th-century Britain.(n)

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The Scottish chefs are at the halfway point of their heat, serving up their mains and desserts celebrating children's literature. One of the contestants attempts an ambitious nose-to-tail rabbit dish as a homage to Toad's feasting in The Wind in the Willows, while another takes inspiration from Dundee's comic hero Desperate Dan and tries to replicate the cowboy's famous cow pie using Scottish beef fillet, beef cheeks, bone marrow rissole and puff pastry. Presented by Susan Calman(n)

programme.title: Hospital
programme.desc: The final episode looks at radical new treatments and surgeries offering cancer patients hopes of survival, including two-year-old Yeshua, who has a tumour in his abdomen. In Iraq, where he was born, Yeshua was given a five per cent chance of survival. Though high doses of chemotherapy have shrunk the tumour over the past six months, in order for Yeshua to survive he needs an extremely complicated 12-hour operation at Alder Hey Children's Hospital to remove as much of the growth as possible(n)

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Glaswegian comedian Frankie Boyle introduces young stand-up sensation Jack Carroll, who rose to fame on Britain's Got Talent in 2013. Holly Walsh will also be taking to the stage at London's Hammersmith Apollo(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Kirsty Wark(n)

programme.title: Growing Up Gifted
programme.desc: Part one of two. Return of the documentary following three gifted teenagers, Liam, Kian and Jamarley, as they prepare to finish secondary school and make important decisions about their futures. Kian dreams of becoming a theoretical physicist, but has to face up to the reality of having to leave Hartlepool to pursue his dreams. In Newcastle, aspiring doctor Liam finds his work ethic is increasingly under scrutiny, while in London, Jamarley faces pressures outside school(n)

programme.title: Growing Up Gifted
programme.desc: Part two of two. The conclusion of the documentary follows three gifted teenage girls, Anne Marie, Jada and Shakira, as they make decisions about their futures. In Port Talbot, Anne Marie is more settled after moving in with her best friend, but isolation from her family and nerves about her exams are starting to trouble her. Meanwhile, Jada prepares for her A Levels at grammar school, and Tamworth-based Shakira has got to make up lost ground if she is to achieve the high grades her teachers have predicted(n)

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Eagles are the most powerful birds in the sky, capable of tackling enormous prey, spotting food at vast distances and soaring for miles on the wing. Armed with cutting-edge filming technology, this documentary examines the science behind such extraordinary abilities. Featuring dramatic stories and some remarkable experiments, the film follows the story of a family of bald eagles in Iowa as two chicks face the challenges of life in the wild. Plus, bird specialist Lloyd Buck puts his trained golden eagle Tilly's skills to the test(n)

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: Another group of six amateur cooks prepare a Signature Dish for judges John Torode and Gregg Wallace, hoping to secure their place in the next stage of the competition. After two contestants are sent home, the remaining four take part in The Three Ingredients Challenge, creating a dish showcasing lamb, aubergine and red pepper(n)

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: Restaurant critic William Sitwell sets the brief for the third quarter-final, challenging the heat-winners to serve up a plant-based dish. John Torode and Gregg Wallace then decide who will be progressing further in the competition and who will be hanging up their aprons(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: At Oxburgh Hall in Norfolk, Anita Manning discovers a Victorian dynamometer used for measuring the pressure of steam and David Harper is taken back to the days of ocean liners with a wine stopper from 1912. Presenter Paul Martin visits Cromer to find out about the RNLI's most decorated lifeboatman(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Hosted by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: Back in Time for...
programme.desc: The Arderns adjust to 1970s life, a time when supermarkets had become Britain's shopping destination of choice and corner shops were beginning to feel the pinch. The family's experience begins with a grand opening designed to compete against the onslaught of the supermarkets. While Sam dons his chicken costume to drum up trade for the shop's 'cheap cheap' prices, the Lord Mayor of Sheffield cuts the ribbon to open the doors. But as a newly self-service operation, Dave must completely transform his approach to keep the tills ringing(n)

programme.title: Coast
programme.desc: An exploration of the history and culture of some of the most extreme locations in Britain. Nick Crane visits the Isles of Scilly, Ruth Goodman walks the dangerous path traversed daily by the Branscombe Cliff farmers in Devon, and Mark Horton investigates the Transatlantic Telegraph service. Meanwhile, Hermione Cockburn learns how the fossil of a large sea creature found at St David's Head, Pembrokeshire, shaped scientists' understanding of Earth's history(n)

programme.title: Hardball
programme.desc: Contestants answer questions to get as far as they can along a track before a ball catches and passes them, ending their go. Quiz, hosted by Ore Oduba(n)

programme.title: Put Your Money Where Your Mouth Is
programme.desc: Antiques experts Eric Knowles and Danny Sebastian meet for the final competition of the week, the showdown. With half their items going to auction, it remains to be seen whether they will be able to turn a profit at all. Eric gets passionate about pottery when he finds a Davenport dish and Danny risks it all at the auction(n)

programme.title: Best Bakes Ever
programme.desc: In light of Britain's baking obsession, actress and cake expert Jane Asher presents a selection of recipes from television chefs, including James Martin, Mary Berry, Paul Hollywood, the Hairy Bikers, Raymond Blanc, Nigella Lawson, Tom Kerridge, Lorraine Pascale and Rachel Khoo(n)

programme.title: The TV That Made Me
programme.desc: Former politician and Strictly star Ann Widdecombe tells Brian Conley about significant TV shows and characters from her past, including sailing soap Howards' Way, music and entertainment show Six-Five Special, Richard Chamberlain as Dr Kildare and 1960 biblical drama Paul of Tarsus(n)

programme.title: Alex Polizzi: Chefs on Trial
programme.desc: The finalists are challenged to produce a three-course wedding menu to be taste-tested by the manager's family and friends. However, the enormity of the task takes its toll, resulting in a burnt main course and undercooked pear tart, and owner Sam has a difficult decision to make before one competitor is given the job of head chef(n)

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Mark Stacey and Michael Baggott assess items brought into Newby Hall in North Yorkshire. Mark comes across an interesting Clarice Cliff cake plate and Michael enjoys an ancient Chinese urn. Presenter Paul Martin takes in the splendours of the country house's Adams interiors(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Lexicographer Susie Dent, newsreader Nick Owen, alpine ski racer Chemmy Alcott and comedian Dane Baptiste test their general knowledge skills, with an overall champion being crowned for the week(n)

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Ben Fogle and Kate Humble meet the diva of the Longleat Park - Inny the giant otter, who is set to be reunited with her old mate Gordito. Elsewhere, avian malaria has decimated the Humboldt colony, with the few surviving birds having gone to other collections. Kate catches up with keepers Georgia and Lucy to reminisce about some of their favourite penguins and their unforgettable antics. Plus, Jean Johansson discovers why koalas can sometimes hamper Australian detectives(n)

programme.title: Rick Stein's Secret France
programme.desc: Rick's tour brings him to Perigord, which is famous for its black truffles and walnuts, as well as duck dishes. He pays a visit to his favourite market in the city of Perigueux, and traces the historic connections between England and the city, which was once known as the Kingdom of Aquitaine. Inspired by his visit, he returns to the kitchen to prepare Enchaud, a traditional pot roast pork from the region, before heading towards the shores of the Mediterranean and the Cote Catalan near the Spanish border(n)

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Host Susan Calman invites the two highest-scoring chefs from Scotland to go head to head and cook their six-course menus again. The duo need to impress formidable judges Matthew Fort, Oliver Peyton and Andi Oliver as well as guest judge David Baddiel, the bestselling children's author and comedian. Presented by Susan Calman(n)

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: The programme joins Adam Frost in his garden to bring viewers inspiration for their plots, along with all the essential gardening jobs for this weekend. Plus, the team presents a guide through seasonal highlights from across the country, visiting stunning gardens, meeting gardeners and finding out their secrets of success.(n)

programme.title: Pilgrimage: The Road to Istanbul
programme.desc: Adrian Chiles, Edwina Currie, Fatima Whitbread, Mim Shaikh, Amar Latif, Dom Joly and Pauline McLynn spend their last day in Serbia at a monastery hidden in the forests of the Sicevo gorge. Before leaving, Adrian leads the group in a ritual of remembrance with each pilgrim leaving a candle for a loved one. The group travels on to Bulgarian capital Sofia, where Mim and Amar explore its only mosque and Dom, Edwina and Fatima visit the museum of socialist art, a memorial to the nation's communist past. The group reunites and follows the trail towards the Rila Mountains, the highest in the Balkans(n)

programme.title: The Mash Report
programme.desc: Home worker Nish Kumar is 'virtually joined' by his quarantined correspondents, including Rachel Parris, Geoff Norcott, Ellie Taylor, Steve N Allen, Catherine Bohart, Desiree Burch and Ahir Shah, for a satirical and surreal take on the week's news(n)

programme.title: The Mini Mash Report
programme.desc: Highlights from the satirical news show, presented by Nish Kumar, as a cast of comedy correspondents give their take on the week's events(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Mark Urban presents analysis of the day's national and international events(n)

programme.title: Midnight Special (2016)
programme.desc: A boy develops strange powers that he cannot understand, attracting the attention of religious extremists. The boy's father is determined to keep him safe, and together they go on the run from the fanatics in the hope of finding out the true purpose of his abilities - but they attract the attention of government agents, who have their own plans for the child. Sci-fi adventure, starring Michael Shannon, Jaeden Lieberher and Kirsten Dunst(n)

programme.title: Coronavirus: Your Essential Update
programme.desc: The latest reports about Covid-19. Presented by Rachel Burden(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Richard Bilton investigates the financial impact of Covid-19, following the workers, supermarkets and manufacturers struggling to survive as food runs short, jobs are lost and panic sets in, telling the story of the fight to save the economy from an unprecedented threat.(n)

programme.title: Murder 24/7
programme.desc: Police and specialists investigate a double stabbing in Colchester's homeless community that leaves Carl Hopkins dead and the other victim seriously injured. Detectives initially question whether the men had attacked one other, and take to the streets to search for witnesses, but people are reluctant to talk(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Wild & Weird
programme.desc: Featuring a group of dogs riding Russia's underground rail system, and a clever crow that can use a pedestrian crossing to crack a tough problem(n)

programme.title: The Dengineers
programme.desc: 11-year-old Holly needs a relaxing den to help her forget about her actinic dermatitis(n)

programme.title: All Over the Place
programme.desc: Ed Petrie and friends go on a road trip across the UK(n)

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: Long-running children's magazine programme(n)

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall takes a boat ride up the Amazon to come face to face with a black piranha. He also encounters electric eels, meets a maned wolf and braves crocodile-infested waters(n)

programme.title: Astronauts: Do You Have What It Takes?
programme.desc: Twelve people compete against each other through six weeks of astronaut training, with the winner receiving support in pursuing a career with a space agency. Former astronaut Chris Hadfield, medical researcher Dr Kevin Fong and psychologist Dr Iya Whiteley oversee the challenges, and can eliminate contenders from the show at any time. In the first episode, they must learn to hover a helicopter(n)

programme.title: Tinker Bell and the Secret of the Wings (2012)
programme.desc: The fairy becomes curious about the mysterious winter forest where fairies never dare to go. She travels into the cold woods, where she meets a new friend and uncovers a threat to both their homes that they must work together to prevent. Disney animated adventure, with the voices of Mae Whitman, Lucy Hale, Anjelica Huston and Timothy Dalton(n)

programme.title: Birds of Paradise: The Ultimate Quest
programme.desc: Part one of two. Thirty years ago explorer Benedict Allen lived in Papua New Guinea with the Niowra, a remote people. Broadcast journalist Frank Gardner has always wished to see wild birds of paradise, so Benedict resolves to take him along. The duo set out through some of the most inhospitable terrain on the planet, negotiating swamps, mountains and crocodile-infested waters, heading into the cloud forest. Despite Frank requiring the use of a wheelchair following a shooting incident in 2004, Benedict determines to get him to their destination(n)

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: As the competition continues, chefs Gordon Jones, Roberta Hall, Ross Bryans and Amy Elles compete in the heats for Scotland. Each chef prepares a starter and fish course celebrating iconic food from children's literature, including a venison tartare with smoked celeriac remoulade which pays homage to J K Rowling's Harry Potter franchise, and a Wind in the Willows inspired dish featuring a sourdough baguette and chicken terrine(n)

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The Scottish chefs are at the halfway point of their heat, serving up their mains and desserts celebrating children's literature. One of the contestants attempts an ambitious nose-to-tail rabbit dish as a homage to Toad's feasting in The Wind in the Willows, while another takes inspiration from Dundee's comic hero Desperate Dan and tries to replicate the cowboy's famous cow pie using Scottish beef fillet, beef cheeks, bone marrow rissole and puff pastry. Presented by Susan Calman(n)

programme.title: The £100K House: Tricks of the Trade
programme.desc: Piers Taylor helps a family build an extension on their ex-council house in Warwick, suggesting that they ditch their ambitious plan and try a simpler one. He designs clever storage, helps them find a low-cost solution for the floor, and creates a multi-coloured porch that would normally cost thousands. Meanwhile, Cheryl Pilliner-Reeves comes to the aid of Karen and David in Ipswich, who are desperate to fall in love with their Victorian terrace, which has a mix of levels and corridors. She must find a way to connect it all, and creates two bespoke staircases for just £600, as well as helping Karen get her dream kitchen(n)

programme.title: Hugh's Wild West
programme.desc: Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall's tour of the West Country continues as he visits the Dorset-Devon border - his home territory. Here, he investigates efforts to bring beavers back to the region for the first time since the 16th century. At Abbotsbury Swannery, resident swan herd Dave Wheeler rounds up his charges for a routine health check, and bat enthusiast Colin Morris offers Hugh an insight into why he transformed a former mansion into a plush sanctuary for Dorset's greater horseshoe bats(n)

programme.title: The Muppets (2011)
programme.desc: Three fans of the famous puppet variety show discover a powerful businessman is going to knock down the Muppet theatre and drill for oil on the site. They track down frontman Kermit the frog and persuade him to reunite his co-stars, so they can put on one last performance to save the venue. Comedy, starring Jason Segel, Amy Adams and Chris Cooper, with songs by Bret McKenzie (Flight of the Conchords)(n)

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: A look back at TV appearances by stars of the silver screen from the 1970s, using archive footage to tell the story of their lives and careers(n)

programme.title: Dad's Army
programme.desc: The platoon takes part in an exercise with the regular army and everything goes well - until pompous Captain Mainwaring assumes the role of an enemy agent on the run. Comedy, starring Arthur Lowe and Clive Dunn. First shown in 1974(n)

programme.title: Australia: Earth's Magical Kingdom
programme.desc: An exploration of how native creatures have adapted to survive in the human environment. In Melbourne, tree-dwelling possums have taken to sharing trees in city parks, risking encounters with traffic and cats. In Brisbane's parks, over the past 150 years of so, eastern water dragons have become twice the size of their wild cousins and without trees to climb their limbs are getting longer and claws shorter. Beyond the cities, dingoes eek out an existence in the inhospitable environment of a desert goldmine, while climate change and deforestation is posing a threat to koalas. Narrated by Barry Humphries(n)

programme.title: Great Asian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo sets off on the first leg of a new quest as he travels around South-east Asia, guided by his 1913 Bradshaw's Handbook on a 2,500-mile railway adventure across six countries. Beginning in Hong Kong, the former politician investigates how Britain won the island and Kowloon from China after two mid-19th century wars over the trade in opium. He also boards the island's most famous funicular to the Peak and visits the state-of-the art West Kowloon station with its high-speed link to China - the largest underground high-speed station in the world(n)

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig looks at 'Quantity' and 'Quality', inviting Bridget Christie, Joe Lycett and James Acaster to join regular panellist Alan Davies to face her fiendish questions. As usual, the host doesn't particularly care whether the answers are right or not, so long as they are interesting(n)

programme.title: Titian - Behind Closed Doors
programme.desc: The story behind the artist's commission for Philip II of Spain, known as the poesie paintings, which had not been seen together for over 450 years and had been the subject of an exhibition at London's National Gallery before it was closed due to the Covid-19 outbreak. Cameras were recently granted access to bring this groundbreaking exhibition to the screens across Britain and beyond(n)

programme.title: Ghost Stories (2017)
programme.desc: Professor Philip Goodman, famous for debunking hoaxes and ghost sightings, is given a dossier of three terrifying, unsolvable cases by his hero and role model Charles Cameron, who has been missing, presumed dead, for years. Can Goodman explain what even Cameron could not? Horror anthology, starring Andy Nyman, Martin Freeman and Paul Whitehouse(n)

programme.title: The Wonder of Animals
programme.desc: Chris Packham explores the ingenious ways in which ants have collaborated to colonise the planet like no other animal. Natural air conditioning systems keep them cool in their nests, shelters made from their own bodies protect them from the elements, and a strong sense of smell helps them to overpower animals hundreds of times larger than themselves. Plus, a look at recent research that reveals how ant colonies are capable of immunising themselves against certain diseases(n)

programme.title: How to Make
programme.desc: Designer and materials engineer Zoe Laughlin dismantles and dissects three classic items to understand the wonders of form, function and material that go into making them, before building her own truly bespoke versions step by step. She begins with modern classic the training shoe, meeting some of the UK's leading designers and manufacturers, as well as young inventors working on sustainable creations such as material made by bacteria and self-deodorising fabrics(n)

programme.title: Mark Kermode's Secrets of Cinema
programme.desc: The critic enters a deadly world of jet-setting espionage, double agents and sinister conspiracies as he decodes that most thrilling of genres - the spy movie(n)

programme.title: Pinewood: 80 Years of Movie Magic
programme.desc: Jonathan Ross gains unprecedented access to the iconic British film studio, meeting Joan Collins and Barbara Windsor to discuss their experiences on set. The site in Buckinghamshire was transformed into a studio during the 1930s and became home to the James Bond, Harry Potter, Superman and Carry On film series, and Ross goes behind the scenes to tackle some daring and spectacular stunts of his own(n)

programme.title: Score: Cinema's Greatest Soundtracks
programme.desc: Hollywood composers including Hans Zimmer, James Cameron, Danny Elfman, John Williams, Quincy Jones and Trent Reznor combine to give a privileged look inside the musical challenges and creative secrecy of the film score(n)

programme.title: Our Classical Century
programme.desc: Suzy Klein and Alexandra Burke explore the world of classical music from the 1980s until the present day when the walls between classical and popular culture began to come down. Classical music and opera were the soundtrack of great national moments of joy and sadness, of entertainment and mourning, and also became accessible to millions through the advent of new technologies. Featuring performances by Nigel Kennedy and Steven Isserlis, and contributions from Sir Simon Rattle, Vanessa-Mae and Torvill and Dean(n)

programme.title: Cinema Through the Eye of Magnum Photos
programme.desc: The photographic studios' famous images of movie stars, from Eve Arnold's intimate relationship with Marilyn Monroe to Paolo Pellegrin's portraits of Kate Winslet(n)

programme.title: The Wonder of Animals
programme.desc: Chris Packham explores the ingenious ways in which ants have collaborated to colonise the planet like no other animal. Natural air conditioning systems keep them cool in their nests, shelters made from their own bodies protect them from the elements, and a strong sense of smell helps them to overpower animals hundreds of times larger than themselves. Plus, a look at recent research that reveals how ant colonies are capable of immunising themselves against certain diseases(n)

programme.title: How to Make
programme.desc: Designer and materials engineer Zoe Laughlin dismantles and dissects three classic items to understand the wonders of form, function and material that go into making them, before building her own truly bespoke versions step by step. She begins with modern classic the training shoe, meeting some of the UK's leading designers and manufacturers, as well as young inventors working on sustainable creations such as material made by bacteria and self-deodorising fabrics(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1989
programme.desc: Mark Goodier and Simon Parkin host the edition first shown on June 15, featuring performances by Fuzzbox, REM, Sinitta, Double Trouble & Rebel MC, Donna Allen, Cyndi Lauper, Jason Donovan and Tom Petty(n)

programme.title: Abba at the BBC
programme.desc: A collection of memorable performances by the group. Agnetha Faltskog, Bjorn Ulvaeus, Benny Andersson and Anni-Frid Lyngstad were thrust into the limelight in 1974 when Waterloo triumphed at the Eurovision Song Contest in Brighton, marking the start of their rise to fame. Various outings were captured by BBC cameras over the next few years and several are featured here alongside their Top of the Pops debut, archive interviews and fly-on-the-wall footage. There is also a chance to see the foursome on a 1982 edition of Noel Edmonds' Late, Late Breakfast Show, which was Abba's last televised appearance in the UK(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1989
programme.desc: Simon Mayo and Gary Davies host the edition first shown on June 22, featuring performances by Living in a Box, the Beautiful South, Gladys Knight, Clannad & Bono, Placido Domingo & Jennifer Rush, Mob featuring LRS, Soul II Soul and the Bangles(n)

programme.title: Rich Hall's Countrier Than You
programme.desc: The American comedian, writer and musician explores the early origins of country music in Nashville and Austin. He visits the rustic studios where this much-loved sound was born and discovers how this genre of music has reinvented itself with influences from bluegrass, western swing and Americana. Rich examines how the music industries differ between these two cities and how they generated their own distinct twist on the genre - such as cosmic country, redneck country and the outlaw artists of the 1970s(n)

programme.title: Sings the Great American Songbook
programme.desc: A compilation of archive performances of hits composed by American songwriters including Cole Porter, Irving Berlin, Johnny Mercer and Harold Arlen. Featuring music by Shirley Bassey, Bryan Ferry, Captain Sensible, Jamie Cullum and Florence and the Machine(n)

programme.title: Sinatra: All or Nothing at All
programme.desc: Memories of Frank Sinatra's legendary 1971 'retirement concert' in Los Angeles are evoked through archived interviews featuring the singer and those close to him, as this documentary series detailing the life, music and career of the entertainer continues(n)

programme.title: Music Moguls: Masters of Pop
programme.desc: The secret history of pop and rock from the men and women who pull the strings behind the scenes. The first of three programmes tells the story of the maverick managers who controlled the careers of major artists, from Colonel Tom Parker (Elvis) up to Scooter Braun (Justin Bieber). Along the way are tales of industry legends such as Led Zeppelin's Peter Grant, and Don Arden, who managed the Small Faces, Black Sabbath and ELO. Contributors include Andrew Loog Oldham, Jon Landau, Bill Curbishley, Paul McGuinness and Jonathan Dickins(n)

programme.title: Rich Hall's Countrier Than You
programme.desc: The American comedian, writer and musician explores the early origins of country music in Nashville and Austin. He visits the rustic studios where this much-loved sound was born and discovers how this genre of music has reinvented itself with influences from bluegrass, western swing and Americana. Rich examines how the music industries differ between these two cities and how they generated their own distinct twist on the genre - such as cosmic country, redneck country and the outlaw artists of the 1970s(n)

programme.title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends
programme.desc: The chef visits Palermo in northern Sicily, a city echoing Arab, North African, French, Spanish and Italian influences, making its cuisine unique and irresistible. Pine nuts, raisins, almonds and golden breadcrumbs are combined with flavours of sun-ripened tomatoes, lemons and the finest olive oil to create a range of unforgettable dishes. Rick witnesses locals celebrating a festival in honour of Palermo's patron saint Rosalia, and receives a cookery lesson from a real-life duchess. At home he cooks Marsala chicken, a Sicilian classic served in UK bistros since the 1960s(n)

programme.title: Coast
programme.desc: Neil Oliver and Tessa Dunlop continue their comprehensive guide to Britain's coastline, this time turning attention to the North Sea coast, a stretch that takes in two nations, and is bordered by a pair of huge estuaries, the Forth in Scotland, and the Humber in England. Neil sheds light on a military shipbuilding project in Rosyth, and heads to the island of Inchmickery to investigate a bird colony with a difference. From there, he tells a tragic story that still haunts the fishing village of Eyemouth, and then visits the holy island of Lindisfarne to make a dramatic archeological discovery(n)

programme.title: Twin
programme.desc: Erik lives alone in a run-down campervan on a desolate beach, while his twin Adam is the complete opposite - a successful man with money, a thriving family business, a loving wife and two children. When Erik's irresponsible lifestyle finally catches up with him, he contacts his brother for help, but Adam brutally rejects him - with fatal consequences. Norwegian crime thriller, starring Kristofer Hivju(n)

programme.title: Twin
programme.desc: As she takes in what she has done, Ingrid realises that she risks losing both home and family. In desperation, she begins to lie about the tragedy, and when the police show up, she quickly winds up in a web of deceit. Norwegian crime thriller, starring Kristofer Hivju and Rebekka Nystabakk(n)

programme.title: Twin Sisters: A World Apart
programme.desc: The story of identical twin girls Alexandra and Mia, who were adopted separately from China, with one growing up in California, and the other in Sweden. A chance sighting at the orphanage prompted the adoptive parents to suspect the girls were related, and a DNA test proved their theory. The film follows the children as they meet in Norway aged eight years old, and captures the similarities between the pair, despite having grown up thousands of miles apart(n)

programme.title: Weatherman Walking
programme.desc: On a route from Pwll to Kidwelly, Derek Brockway turns Pied Piper as he launches a walk of 800 children marking the anniversary of 'People Power' that saved a stretch of the Loughor Estuary for families to enjoy. On this Carmarthenshire coastal walk, the weatherman also discovers a former munitions factory that employed 6,000 people, has a spin in a racing car and learns the story Wales' feisty warrior princess Gwenllian(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: World darts champion Peter Wright, Paralympic great Sarah Storey, former Premier League striker Dion Dublin and ex-England cricketer Steve Harmison join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz(n)

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: Ten talented amateur cooks keen to reach the semi-final stage are challenged to make canapes or party food, with nine identical portions of a dish in miniature that can be either sweet or savoury. Meanwhile characters Max and Paddy dress up as sailors in an effort to meet some ladies(n)

programme.title: Rhod Gilbert's Work Experience
programme.desc: Rhod is thrown in at the deep ends as he joins the rigorous Royal Navy training scheme - which isn't good news for a man who struggles with seasickness. He is also given a chance to take the helm of one of Her Majesty's warships - only to take this crash-course in marine navigation all too literally(n)

programme.title: Young, Welsh and Pretty Minted
programme.desc: Football vlogger Gonth decides he wants to be a TV presenter, while Mima gives up her day job as a graphic designer to risk it all to go into wedding fashion(n)

programme.title: Miranda
programme.desc: The shop owner's friends accuse her of being immature, so she sets out to prove she can officially be an adult. First she looks after a toddler - only to find the lure of the soft-play centre just too tempting - and then hosts a dinner party for eight, where disaster is on the menu. Comedy, starring Miranda Hart, Tom Ellis and Patricia Hodge(n)

programme.title: London Kills

programme.title: Breakfast

programme.title: The Snow Spider
programme.desc: On his ninth birthday, Gwyn Griffiths' grandmother bestows him with five special gifts infused with ancient Welsh magic. Following her instructions, he climbs the snowy Pendewi mountains where his sister Bethan went missing and is amazed to discover the luminous Snow Spider. Children's fantasy drama based on Jenny Nimmo's trilogy, starring Fflyn Edwards(n)

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests review the week's headlines and look ahead to forthcoming fixtures in the Premier League, Football League and Scottish Premiership. Plus, interviews with some of the leading names in the game and features on teams from all levels of football(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day Classic: Wales v Germany 1991
programme.desc: In 1991 the World Champions Germany came to Cardiff to take on Wales in a European Championship qualifier. This classic match would go down as one of the great days in Welsh football.(n)

programme.title: Blue Planet Revisited
programme.desc: In the first of two programmes, Steve Backshall discovers why the Bahamas provide a sanctuary for sharks, looking at the protection offered to them, people's changing attitudes and the key role science plays in their conservation. He joins researchers who have dedicated their lives to this unique and often misunderstood group of animals and learns that a healthy shark population is absolutely vital to a healthy marine ecosystem(n)

programme.title: A to Z of TV Gardening
programme.desc: Carol Kirkwood takes a journey through the world of horticulture, getting inspiration and advice from some of the BBC's most popular gardening presenters. The series progresses in alphabetical order from A to Z, with each edition featuring themes beginning with a different letter(n)

Starring Shaheen Jafargholi and George Rainsford(n)

programme.title: Gavin & Stacey
programme.desc: First episode of the comedy about the romance between a couple with differing cultures and backgrounds. Mathew Horne and Joanna Page star alongside Rob Brydon, Alison Steadman, Ruth Jones and James Corden(n)

programme.title: Outnumbered

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Danny and Steve 'welcome' new boss Ted Case into the UCOS office in Sasha's absence, but for all his experience, keen eye for detail and a killer instinct for detecting liars, they aren't as sold on his superstitious quirks. While trying to work one another out, the team treads carefully in the case of a murdered vicar who received racist hate mail in the lead up to his death. With emotions running high, it's up to Ted to pull together his new unit and keep the peace in one of UCOS's most sensitive cases. Starring Larry Lamb, Denis Lawson and Nicholas Lyndhurst(n)

programme.desc: The man who knew the fate of DI Bradford's wife is found dead. Detective drama, starring Hugo Speer, Sharon Small, Bailey Patrick and Tori Allen-Martin(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah is in Shropshire with a garlic specialist and his Russian wife who want to establish a new business that taps into the county's thriving food scene, though not before securing their dream family home in the country on a £550,000 budget. While in the county, Sonali gets in tune with the region's melodic heritage when she learns about the expert craft of harp-making(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: In Witley, Surrey, Sarah Moore calls on blacksmith Kev Paxton to revamp a pair of metal bed ends, while upholsterer Ray Clarke gives an old armchair a complete redesign. For her own transformation, Sarah has a chest of drawers carcass to repurpose(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Antique experts Christina Trevanion and Charles Hanson are on the last leg of their coast-to-coast journey from Morecambe to Newcastle. Christina buys some quirky curios, while Charles splurges on a multitude of items including a Roman-era ring that he titles the Hanson Hoard. The road trip pit crew leaps into action when smoke appears inside the classic car, and all fingers point to Charles for this minor mishap. Plus, Christina detours to visit Northampton, the shoemaking capital of Britain(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: BBC News at Six; Weather
programme.desc: Presented by Sally Magnusson(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Alex Scott host the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: World darts champion Peter Wright, Paralympic great Sarah Storey, former Premier League striker Dion Dublin and ex-England cricketer Steve Harmison join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz(n)

programme.title: Miranda
programme.desc: The shop owner's friends accuse her of being immature, so she sets out to prove she can officially be an adult. First she looks after a toddler - only to find the lure of the soft-play centre just too tempting - and then hosts a dinner party for eight, where disaster is on the menu. Comedy, starring Miranda Hart, Tom Ellis and Patricia Hodge(n)

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: Ten talented amateur cooks keen to reach the semi-final stage are challenged to make canapes or party food, with nine identical portions of a dish in miniature that can be either sweet or savoury. The stakes are high because at the end one contestants will be sent home and their dream of raising the 2020 trophy will be over. The remaining nine will embark on the next step of the competition. Presented by John Torode and Gregg Wallace(n)

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Former Breakfast and Watchdog presenter Steph McGovern hosts as the satirical quiz returns for its 59th series. Journalist Helen Lewis and comedian Miles Jupp join regular team leaders Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's events(n)

programme.title: Peter Kay's Comedy Shuffle
programme.desc: More clips documenting the much-loved comedy star's best work from his decades in showbusiness. Peter makes another erratic appearance on Jonathan Ross' show, while there's a special appearance by Sheridan Smith and the Script. Meanwhile characters Max and Paddy dress up as sailors in an effort to meet some ladies(n)

programme.title: Sliding Doors (1998)
programme.desc: A London woman's entire future hinges on whether or not she catches a train. In one version of events, she gets the chance to start afresh and lead a happy new life, but in the other she is stuck supporting her lazy, unfaithful boyfriend. Comedy drama, presenting two parallel stories featuring the same character. Starring Gwyneth Paltrow, John Hannah and John Lynch(n)

programme.title: London Kills
programme.desc: The man who knew the fate of DI Bradford's wife is found dead. Detective drama, starring Hugo Speer, Sharon Small, Bailey Patrick and Tori Allen-Martin(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chef Tom Brown, and a special guest faces his or her food heaven or hell. Plus, archive clips from Rick Stein and Keith Floyd(n)

programme.title: Nadiya's Family Favourites
programme.desc: Nadiya Hussain demonstrates quick recipes for days when everything is a bit hectic, including mango and lime lassi bircher, a spin on prawn toast, seafood crumble, and a simple apple Rocky Road recipe. Nadiya's love for the simplicity of cereal takes her to the world's first cereal café in London, and her appreciation for bread sees her meet passionate baker Heather and the members of her club(n)

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests review the week's headlines and look ahead to forthcoming fixtures in the Premier League, Football League and Scottish Premiership. Plus, interviews with some of the leading names in the game and features on teams from all levels of football(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts and the team explore properties about to be auctioned, then talk to the buyers about their plans for the buildings(n)

programme.title: Rugby League: Challenge Cup Classics
programme.desc: Mark Chapman presents highlights of five memorable finals - Leeds v St Helens from 1978, Wigan v Hull FC from 1985, St Helens v Bradford Bulls from 1996, Bradford Bulls v Leeds Rhinos from 2003 and Hull FC v Warrington Wolves from 2016(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning looks after proceedings today from two antiques centres in the Hertfordshire town of Sawbridgeworth, joined by experts John Cameron and Caroline Hawley(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin helps an expat couple who want to return from Cyprus to buy a house in Lincolnshire, and also visits a college to find out about the next generation of farmers(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore is in Surrey, checking out the cast-offs at Witley Recycling Centre, where she comes across a drum kit to repurpose(n)

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present as famous faces take part in the general knowledge quiz in which they try to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys tests four more celebrities in the famous black chair. Taking part tonight Paralympic alpine skier Kelly Gallagher, comedian and presenter Stephen Bailey, actor and comedy writer Daniel Lawrence Taylor, and presenter and mathematician Bobby Seagull(n)

programme.title: Catchpoint
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness hosts a family-friendly game show combining physical fun and an exciting question mechanic, as contestants have to think fast as they answer questions while trying to catch balls of various sizes dropped from the studio ceiling, with a variety of entertaining rounds and a cash prize at stake(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Marty finally acknowledges how dangerous his behaviour has been outside of work and decides to change his ways. Meanwhile, Ethan questions what he really wants when Fenisha turns him down, and David suggests that Dylan's behaviour indicates feelings towards Faith, but Dylan denies it. Starring Shaheen Jafargholi and George Rainsford(n)

programme.title: Gavin & Stacey
programme.desc: First episode of the comedy about the romance between a couple with differing cultures and backgrounds. Gavin and Stacey have flirted for months on the phone at work but have never met up - until she travels from Wales to London to see him. Mathew Horne and Joanna Page star alongside Rob Brydon, Alison Steadman, Ruth Jones and James Corden(n)

programme.title: Outnumbered
programme.desc: The Brockmans have to make some tough decisions about work and video games, as well as who to take to a family funeral - an event that has the vicar wishing none of them had ever come. Comedy, starring Hugh Dennis, Claire Skinner and Tyger Drew-Honey(n)

programme.title: All Round to Mrs Brown's
programme.desc: Mrs Brown's Boys creator, writer and star Brendan O'Carroll dons Agnes's famous cardigan and curly wig once again as he throws open the doors of the larger-than-life Irish matriarch's home to host the Saturday night chat show. In the third episode of the series, Agnes and the family are joined on the show by Spice Girl and girl power icon Mel B, comedy actor and Little Britain star Matt Lucas, with his mother Diana Lobatto. Plus, a special musical performance from Jax Jones and Raye(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker is joined by Alan Shearer and Ian Wright for a discussion on the top ten goalkeepers in Premier League history(n)

programme.title: Source Code (2011)
programme.desc: A military pilot is the subject of a top-secret experiment that transfers his mind into the body of a man killed in a terrorist bombing on a train, but only for the last eight minutes of the dead man's life. His mission is to use that time to find out who is responsible and if they intend to strike again. Sci-fi thriller, starring Jake Gyllenhaal, Michelle Monaghan, Vera Farmiga and Jeffrey Wright(n)

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. As the United States grapples with a full-blown health crisis, ITV's Washington Correspondent Robert Moore asks whether his controversial leadership style has properly addressed or even exacerbated the challenge ahead(n)

programme.title: The Martin Lewis Money Show
programme.desc: The financial journalist presents money-saving tips to help consumers cope with the current economic climate, offering expert advice on everything from premium bonds, student loans and tax credits to supermarket loyalty schemes and high-street shopping(n)

programme.title: Secrets of the Driving Test
programme.desc: An insight into the world of the driving test, filmed over a two-year period and offering unprecedented access to the Driver and Vehicle Standards Agency (DVSA), with cameras fitted inside and outside of the test car. In the first episode, examiner Wayne has the task of taking 27-year-old Shaunna from Hull through the driving test, which will be her 12th attempt at passing. In Cardiff, Drew is desperate to pass, if only to stop the arguments with his partner Karl, who's been teaching him to drive. Narrated by Alan Carr(n)

programme.title: Gordon, Gino and Fred: American Road Trip
programme.desc: Having previously toured their own countries, Gordon Ramsay, Gino D'Acampo and Fred Sirieix now hit the highways of of the States - although in the first episode, they are in Mexico, on a mission to taste the world's best breakfast. Along the way, the trio take part in a chili-eating competition, have a beach buggy race and watch some traditional Mexican wrestling - but it isn't long before they are persuaded not take part(n)

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents(n)

programme.title: Heathrow: Britain's Busiest Airport
programme.desc: Documentary going behind the scenes of the airport, finding out how its infrastructure helps staff process thousands of passengers arriving from 84 countries. This time, Security Officer Estefania investigates an oddly shaped object, and Passenger Experience Manager Harry deals with a medical emergency. Elsewhere, Border Force Bob helps a circus performer with visa troubles(n)

programme.title: Liar
programme.desc: Andrew is pushed to the edge and as he runs out of options, he concocts a plan to take drastic measures. But first, he will need help. In the present day, a vital piece of evidence comes to light and Laura seeks to discover its origin. Now in a desperate position, she must use all her wits to get the information she needs, but with Katy and Liam's assistance, she is led to a startling new suspect. Thriller, starring Joanne Froggatt and Ioan Gruffudd, with Zoe Tapper, Richie Campbell and Katherine Kelly(n)

programme.title: Belgravia
programme.desc: Anne and James are at loggerheads following the revelations at the Brockenhursts' party. With Oliver fixated on discovering the origins of his father's new protege, Susan uses the opportunity to plan a covert trip to meet with a new friend, and enlists her maid Speer to keep the true nature of the rendezvous under wraps. Meanwhile, Lady Templemore begins work on her lavish plans for her daughter's wedding, but much to her disappointment, Lady Maria seems fixated on someone other than her fiance(n)

Drama based on the book by Ann Cleeves, starring Brenda Blethyn and David Leon(n)

programme.desc: A paranormal expert moves into a haunted house where his daughter befriends the amiable ghost of a young boy - but things start to go awry when three smelly spooks and two dastardly crooks try to chase the new residents away. Family comedy, starring Christina Ricci, Bill Pullman, Eric Idle, Cathy Moriarty and featuring the voice of Malachi Pearson(n)

programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four celebrity contestants pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. programme.title: Ant & Dec's Saturday Night Takeaway
programme.desc: The popular duo's entertainment show, featuring undercover pranks, surprises galore, star guests and a chance for a member of the audience to win the items advertised in one of last week's commercial breaks. programme.title: Inception (2010)
programme.desc: A talented thief uses hi-tech devices to enter other people's dreams so he can steal their secrets. Even DS Maddox isn't getting on Hathaway's nerves quite as much, so this rejuvenated trio sets out to discover who murdered American classics student Rose Anderson, whose body has been hauled from the canal with neck and abdomen wounds. Nevertheless, a few red herrings threaten to get in their way of landing the killer. Kevin Whately, Laurence Fox and Angela Griffin star(n)

Having been the driver involved in the fatal collision with Cal Pritchard, Janet is under investigation and unable to interview the man's widow Evie. Devastated by the consequences of her actions, the detective eventually finds solace in Chris's calm and kind reasoning, but when an incandescent Evie smells alcohol on Gill's breath, she has all the ammunition she needs to discredit the case and bring the DCI down(n)

programme.desc: Deirdre stays by Tracy's bedside all night. Gail drinks heavily in the Rovers and storms out when Martin arrives. Deirdre gives Samir some shocking news(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Ken supports Deirdre in her decision to give Tracy one of her kidneys. Vera is shocked to discover Don and Josie spent the night together(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Two couples disappear in the tiny village of Monks Barton amid rumours of witchcraft and haunted woods. As Barnaby and Jones investigate, psychic Cyrus LeVanu clashes with cleric Wallace Stone over their different methods of 'healing' the woods, while other locals seem to be caught up in a stolen antiques racket. When a body is unearthed and the death toll rises, the detectives race to catch the killer by finding a connection between the missing couples and the thefts. Anton Lesser guest stars, with John Nettles and Jason Hughes(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Carol calls the police when she discovers Eva and James Knight's cottage has been vandalised, but the couple are reluctant to involve the law as Eva believes they are being targeted because she is Jewish. Meanwhile, an ancient traction steam engine called Goliath causes chaos in Aidensfield when it breaks down, and further mayhem arises when David takes it for a test run. Deborah Findlay and Tim Woodward guest(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Wealthy industrialist Sir Reuben Astwell is murdered after making a deal to sell synthetic rubber to the Germans, leaving the dapper sleuth wondering whether the motive was commercial or political. Evidence found in his chemical factory could hold the key to the mystery - but Poirot is unsure if the victim's widow can be trusted. Starring David Suchet, with Ann Bell and Bill Wallis(n)

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: First episode of the comedy drama series starring Martin Clunes as Dr Martin Ellingham, an eminent surgeon forced to retrain as a GP after developing an aversion to blood. Learning a vacancy has arisen in the sleepy Cornish village of Portwenn, Martin promptly applies and gets the job, despite opposition from local schoolteacher Louisa. With Caroline Catz, Stephanie Cole and Celia Imrie(n)

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: The medic struggles to dissuade the locals from using his surgery as a drop-in centre and finds himself forced to deliver bad news to a former teacher already bitter at losing his job to Louisa. Adding to his woes, the decision to sack incompetent receptionist Elaine results in the entire village ostracising him. Comedy drama, starring Martin Clunes, Caroline Catz and Lucy Punch(n)

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Rachel steps up to acting DI on a case that becomes bigger and more sinister than it first seemed. Meanwhile, Janet is glad of Rachel's return, having not known how much she was going to need her friend. Starring Lesley Sharp and Suranne Jones(n)

programme.title: Changeling (2008)
programme.desc: A single mother in 1920s Los Angeles is distraught when her son goes missing. After months of searching, the police claim to have found him, but she insists the boy is an imposter. She resolves to prove the police are trying to bury the case, but they respond by slandering her as a hysterical and unfit mother, leading to her confinement in a mental hospital. Clint Eastwood's fact-based drama, with Angelina Jolie and John Malkovich(n)

programme.title: The Good Karma Hospital
programme.desc: Lydia and Ruby visit a convent to make a seemingly miraculous diagnosis, but the treatment of a young nun and the senior doctor's interfering attitude creates tension between them, which finally erupts in a fierce confrontation. Maggie and Paul join a group of pilgrims on a trek through the jungle, hoping to find the answer to their prayers. Guest starring Madhur Jaffrey(n)

programme.title: The Good Karma Hospital
programme.desc: The monsoon season is beginning, and as the first rains start to fall, the tension between Lydia and Ruby is reaching boiling point - driving the younger doctor to make a shocking decision. Maggie's health begins to decline dramatically, and Debbie is adamant that the family must return to the UK. However, Maggie remains determined to remain in India for the time she has left, and struggles to persuade her daughter to honour her last wishes. Conclusion of the medical drama, starring Amrita Acharia and Amanda Redman(n)

programme.desc: Text-based information service(n)

programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home(n)

programme.title: Outside Edge
programme.desc: Kevin and Mim meet Maggie for the first time. Cricketing comedy drama, starring Brenda Blethyn(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian detective looks forward to a quiet Christmas, but is disturbed by the Foreign Office when an Egyptian prince loses a famous ruby - after making the mistake of lending it to a mysterious woman. Guest starring Stephanie Cole, Nigel Le Vaillant and Tariq Alibai(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A prestigious golf club is the setting for murder when a player is bludgeoned to death on the 13th hole. Barnaby and Jones discover gambling, illegal money-lending and assault are rife among the snobbish members, who are also dismissive of the local villagers and only allow them on the course for a few hours a week. Before long, another body turns up, making the detectives doubly determined to catch this terror of the fairways. John Nettles and Jason Hughes star, with guest appearances by Nicholas Le Prevost, John Standing and real-life golfer Sam Torrance(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Long-running drama with the residents of England's most famous cobbled street(n)

programme.desc: Long-running drama with the residents of England's most famous cobbled street(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Long-running drama with the residents of England's most famous cobbled street(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A masterpiece by landscape artist Henry Hogson causes a stir when it is auctioned for £400,000 after a fierce bidding war - and hours later, its former owner is tortured and killed. When a Hogson work is then stolen, Barnaby and Jones research the Midsomer painter with the help of art teacher Matilda Simms. They soon uncover evidence of forgery - but the killer is still at large. Guest starring David Bamber and Susannah Harker, with John Nettles and Jason Hughes(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby's secret past as a spy is revealed when a former agent is killed after a cricket match between the villagers and the gentry. The dead man's wounds appear to have been inflicted by a giant claw, triggering rumours of a 'beast of Midsomer', and before long, another body turns up with similar injuries. Barnaby then realises the incidents are linked to the time the local network of spies spent in Cold War Berlin. John Nettles and Jason Hughes star, with guest appearances by Anna Massey, Peter Davison and Neil Stuke(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Jones are called away from a team-building exercise to investigate a fatal explosion at a haulage yard in Calham Cross. The property is owned by Territorial Army commanding officer Matt Parkes, the father of a businessman whose ruthlessness in the boardroom is matched only by his behaviour toward women. To complicate matters, the village is also home to a peace protester and a grieving mother - and Barnaby has to decide if any of these locals is capable of arson and murder. John Nettles and Jason Hughes star, with Tim Pigott-Smith, Joseph Millson, Niamh Cusack, Judy Parfitt and Nick Fletcher(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Two couples disappear in the tiny village of Monks Barton amid rumours of witchcraft and haunted woods. As Barnaby and Jones investigate, psychic Cyrus LeVanu clashes with cleric Wallace Stone over their different methods of 'healing' the woods, while other locals seem to be caught up in a stolen antiques racket. When a body is unearthed and the death toll rises, the detectives race to catch the killer by finding a connection between the missing couples and the thefts. Anton Lesser guest stars, with John Nettles and Jason Hughes(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A disused abbey that is believed to be cursed is about to be turned into a pub, a decision that has angered many superstitious locals - not to mention the real ale society. The day after the launch night, the curse appears to have claimed a victim - a body is found boiled to death in a kettle of beer, in a manner very similar to an infamous execution on the site in the 16th century. Elaine Paige guest stars, while Annette Badland joins the cast as new pathologist Fleur Perkins(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The headmaster of a local school is murdered, his body found pinned to the wall in the manner of his own butterfly collection. The dead man was a mysterious international club for geniuses, which are currently testing the school's pupils for possible new recruits. Barnaby and Winter investigate the secretive organisation, which has proven controversial in the local area, but also uncover the possibility of a connection between the killing and the impact of pesticides on a rare species of butterfly(n)

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Unable to get used to the fact she was second choice for the sergeant's job, Rachel punishes Janet for keeping the truth from her, and although Will cheers her up with the news she has been chosen for a vice initiative, the detective is soon stung by Gill's harsh words. Meanwhile, an unconscious baby is admitted to hospital with injuries that do not match his parents' explanation of events, and when an unregistered childminder and her boyfriend enter the equation, it's unclear who had the boy's best interests at heart(n)

programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Tracy assures Deirdre she's not a drug addict. Gail is prescribed antidepressants by her doctor. Bet offers to give Ken a place to stay at the Rovers(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Gail is sick of everyone talking about her. Tricia is furious to see a for sale sign up outside her house. Martin tells Audrey he's had enough of Gail(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Terry, Vic and Eric set off for France to pick up some cheap booze. The Dingles bring Sam home from prison. Frank apologises to Kim, leaving her feeling guilty(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kim tells Dave that Frank is going away for the night and invites him to come over. Biff starts to worry about his best man's speech. Luke's party at the Woolpack gets under way(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: When the hunting den belonging to master of the hounds Gordon Radford is vandalised there is no shortage of suspects. Lord Ashfordly is seen publicly arguing with Radford, his kennelman Alec Pickering is threatened with dismissal, and farmer Richard Smith promises repercussions for the damage caused to his land. Ray Ashcroft guest stars(n)

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Chrissy and Jo are entranced when they meet Robin's older brother. Comedy, with Richard O'Sullivan(n)

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Robin and his brother lock horns over Chrissy's affections, while Mr Roper tries his hand at chimney sweeping. Comedy, with Richard O'Sullivan(n)

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Rigsby wines and dines Miss Jones in a suit surreptitiously 'borrowed' from stroppy wrestler Spooner - who turns up halfway through the main course intent on exacting revenge. Vintage comedy, starring Leonard Rossiter(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Pawnbroker Peter Lawson is shot dead in an armed robbery at his premises and Frost's investigations unearth a tragic family secret. He is convinced Lawson was killed by one of his children in revenge for years of systematic sexual abuse - but the truth turns out to be even more chilling. David Jason stars, with Diane Keen, Isla Blair, Matt Bardock, Anthony Bate, Mark Lambert and Edward Purver(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Hard-up local employer Albert Ross is suspected of stealing money from a blind man - and as events unfold, it begins to seem increasingly likely he is also putting profits before the welfare of his disgruntled workforce. programme.title: Heartbeat

Meanwhile, David risks his life to save dispirited musician Keith Drake from a watery grave, and inspires him to set about rebuilding his career(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: An archaeologist dies of a heart attack shortly after smashing the seal of an ancient Egyptian tomb, raising the spectre of the mummy's curse. The grieving widow summons the Belgian sleuth to unravel the mystery, but he can only smoke out the real villain by risking his own life. David Suchet stars, with Anna Cropper and Bill Bailey(n)

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Hathaway appears to have got over his fear of Lewis treading over his newly promoted toes. Their opposites have attracted in the past, and there seems no reason to suspect that they won't now the younger man has got used to the idea of his former mentor coming out of retirement to lend him a hand. Even DS Maddox isn't getting on Hathaway's nerves quite as much, so this rejuvenated trio sets out to discover who murdered American classics student Rose Anderson, whose body has been hauled from the canal with neck and abdomen wounds. Nevertheless, a few red herrings threaten to get in their way of landing the killer. Kevin Whately, Laurence Fox and Angela Griffin star(n)

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: The discovery of slaves on the Pritchard farm has turned a murder inquiry into a multi-agency operation. The enormity of the case brings Dodson to Syndicate 9, keen to establish Gill's state of mind and efficacy as senior officer. Having been the driver involved in the fatal collision with Cal Pritchard, Janet is under investigation and unable to interview the man's widow Evie. Devastated by the consequences of her actions, the detective eventually finds solace in Chris's calm and kind reasoning, but when an incandescent Evie smells alcohol on Gill's breath, she has all the ammunition she needs to discredit the case and bring the DCI down(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
